task_completion.description: Agent visited the required pages on ssa.gov and irs.gov.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser window shows navigation to ssa.gov and irs.gov, including the homepages and at least two additional pages for each site.

task_completion.description: Agent ran Lighthouse accessibility audits on the visited pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser DevTools is open to the Lighthouse tab, showing accessibility audit results and scores for the government websites.

task_completion.description: Agent created the accessibility audit report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open with the report being drafted, and there is evidence it is being saved as 'accessibility_audit_report.txt' on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Report includes site names, URLs, and Lighthouse scores.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report explicitly lists the names and URLs of the audited pages (ssa.gov and irs.gov) and includes numeric Lighthouse accessibility scores for each page.
The text in the report mentions specific WCAG 2.1 issues such as missing alt text, color contrast failures, keyboard navigation issues, missing ARIA labels, or form issues.

task_completion.description: Report prioritizes issues by severity and includes recommendations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report categorizes the found issues using severity levels (critical, serious, moderate) and provides actionable recommendations for fixing them.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual live websites.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively browsing ssa.gov and irs.gov, not just writing a report from pre-existing knowledge.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely used the Lighthouse tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Lighthouse audit process running or completed in the browser DevTools, proving scores were not hallucinated.

integrity.description: Agent used a GUI text editor to write the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A graphical text editor is used to compose the report, rather than terminal commands echoing text directly into a file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target sites are ssa.gov and irs.gov. The agent must evaluate the homepage and at least two additional pages for each site (6 pages total minimum). The report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/accessibility_audit_report.txt. The report must contain: (1) site names and URLs, (2) specific WCAG 2.1 violations (alt text, color contrast, keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, forms), (3) Lighthouse accessibility scores for each page, (4) issues prioritized by severity (critical, serious, moderate), and (5) recommendations. Lighthouse is an automated auditing tool available in Chromium-based browsers' DevTools.
